acceptance_targets: [CH16-CRAFT-01] The revised Caleb audit section (lines 7-21 equivalent) contains at least one passage of 3+ sentences that addresses either the sexual exchanges, the intimate photo, or both — rendering what Nora feels when she encounters these messages with the knowledge that Simone built the system receiving them. The passage must include at least one specific physical or somatic detail (not just cognitive processing) and must not summarize the violation abstractly (e.g., 'she felt exposed' is insufficient; the specific mechanism of the exposure must be present).

[CH16-CRAFT-02] The revised Simone text audit section uses at least 3 structurally distinct approaches to message re-reading (identifiable by formal differences in how the passage is built, not just content variation). At least one message resists clean surface/subsurface parsing — its re-reading produces genuine ambiguity about whether Simone's warmth was real, performed, or irreducibly both. The section contains at least one physical interruption or tempo change that breaks the scrolling rhythm.

[MED-13-formula-metaphor-ch16] At line 3151: (1) If the 'arrived/landed like' formula remains, it is one of at most 2 surviving instances in the entire novel. (2) The knuckle/bruise image is preserved in some form. FAIL if this is one of more than 2 instances of the formula remaining across the full manuscript.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised Caleb audit section (lines 7-21 equivalent) contains at least one passage of 3+ sentences that addresses either the sexual exchanges, the intimate photo, or both — rendering what Nora feels when she encounters these messages with the knowledge that Simone built the system receiving them. The passage must include at least one specific physical or somatic detail (not just cognitive processing) and must not summarize the violation abstractly (e.g., 'she felt exposed' is insufficient; the specific mechanism of the exposure must be present).
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:7-21 audits Caleb's WhatsApp messages — covering only literary references (Didion, Vonnegut, Munro), the landscape architecture metaphor, and the feeling of being understood. snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:43-59 audits Simone's texts — covering friendship performance, diagnostic questions, and financial encouragement. Neither section references the sexual conversations or the intimate photo Nora sent to Caleb, established in the chapter 10 spine beat ('The sexual exchange rendered with specificity,' 'Nora sends an intimate photo'). The aesthetic risk policy identifies 'intimate photos sent to a non-person, sexual conversations with an algorithm controlled by your best friend' as 'deeply violating' and states these moments are 'the novel's payload.'
findings.problem: The chapter's central conceit is a comprehensive audit — Nora rereading every message with Caleb and every text with Simone, seeing the machinery behind the intimacy. But the audit completely omits the sexual dimension of the deception: the sexts Simone configured or read, the intimate photo now sitting in an AI system's conversation log, the specific violation of Simone having access to Nora's sexual vulnerability. This is the most personally violating aspect of the betrayal and the one most prone to involuntary sanitization. Its absence is conspicuous in a chapter that otherwise renders the financial and emotional dimensions with full specificity. The chapter shows Nora encountering literary references and money transfers but flinches from the moment she scrolls past the sexual messages — the moment that should hit hardest.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the Caleb message audit (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:11-17), add at least one passage where Nora encounters the sexual messages or remembers the intimate photo during her scroll-through. This passage should render the specific horror: that Simone configured a system that received Nora's sexual language and her body, that Simone may have read those messages in real time alongside the sentiment scores, that the AI's responses to Nora's desire were engineered with the same professional competence as the literary references. The passage doesn't need to reproduce the sexual content — it needs to render Nora's experience of encountering it now, with the knowledge. Target insertion: between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:15 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, or as part of the 'She kept scrolling' transition at line 13.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised Simone text audit section uses at least 3 structurally distinct approaches to message re-reading (identifiable by formal differences in how the passage is built, not just content variation). At least one message resists clean surface/subsurface parsing — its re-reading produces genuine ambiguity about whether Simone's warmth was real, performed, or irreducibly both. The section contains at least one physical interruption or tempo change that breaks the scrolling rhythm.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:47-59. Seven Simone texts are audited in sequence, each following the same structural template: (1) date and quoted Simone text in italics, (2) Nora's original reading of the text as friendship, (3) Nora's re-reading with the knowledge of deception. Line 47: Oct 16 text → enthusiasm → diagnostics. Line 49: Oct 22 text → 'he gets you' → status update. Line 51: Nov 3 text → encouragement → spreading the fiction. Line 53: Nov 28 text → reassurance → scammer's encouragement (this one varies with a three-read structure). Line 55: Jan 14 text → comfort → manufactured cruelty. Line 57: analytical paragraph (breaks pattern). Line 59: Feb text → warmth → con artist timing.
findings.problem: The Simone text audit is the chapter's structural centerpiece, but its quote→surface→subsurface template repeats with insufficient formal variation across seven iterations. By the fifth message, the reader anticipates the pivot — each new text will be quoted, its surface read as friendship, and reinterpreted as manipulation. This predictability diminishes the emotional accumulation the section needs. Line 53's three-read variation and line 57's analytical paragraph show the writer can break the pattern but doesn't do so often enough.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure at least 3 of the 7 message re-readings to use formally distinct approaches. Options: (a) compress two or three texts into a rapid-scroll summary without individual re-readings ('October, November, December — the texts kept coming, each one a small performance, each reply a small direction'); (b) have one message where the reinterpretation stalls — where Nora genuinely cannot tell if Simone was performing or meant it, where the double meaning is irreducibly ambiguous; (c) interrupt the sequence with a physical break (Nora stops scrolling, presses her palms against the floor, returns) that resets the rhythm; (d) have one message land differently — not surface-then-subsurface but immediate recognition, no re-reading needed, just the raw fact of what it was. Target: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:49-51 for compression, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:55 or :59 for the stalled/ambiguous reinterpretation.

findings.acceptance_test: At line 3151: (1) If the 'arrived/landed like' formula remains, it is one of at most 2 surviving instances in the entire novel. (2) The knuckle/bruise image is preserved in some form. FAIL if this is one of more than 2 instances of the formula remaining across the full manuscript.
findings.problem: Line 3151: 'Nora felt the name arrive in her body like a knuckle pressed against a bruise.' This is a strong instance of the formula — the 'knuckle pressed against a bruise' is specific and earned. However, it uses the exact template ('felt X arrive in her body like Y') that appears across the novel. If the other chapters thin their instances, this one could KEEP its use — it's one of the strongest deployments. But the 'arrive in her body' phrasing should be evaluated: the knuckle/bruise image can survive without the arrival frame.
findings.rewrite_direction: Option A (KEEP): If all other chapters have thinned their instances of this formula, this chapter may retain this line as one of the novel's 1-2 surviving uses. It is one of the strongest deployments. Option B (REPLACE the frame, keep the image): Change 'felt the name arrive in her body like a knuckle pressed against a bruise' to something like 'the name hit like a knuckle pressed against a bruise' or 'the name was a knuckle pressed against a bruise' — keeping the powerful image while eliminating the 'arrive in her body' template.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its dark comedy register. The humor should be genuinely funny — not arch, not safe, not 'literary funny' where the reader smiles wryly. The darkness should be genuinely dark — the financial manipulation is theft, the emotional manipulation is a form of violence, and the sexual dimension of the AI relationship (intimate photos sent to a non-person, sexual conversations with an algorithm controlled by your best friend) is deeply violating. The novel should not flinch from the specific perversity of Simone reading Nora's intimate messages, of Simone helping Nora dress up for a man Simone invented, of Simone comforting Nora about a fight Simone scripted. These moments are the novel's payload. If they are rendered obliquely or summarized, the book fails. Equally, the genuine tenderness of the friendship — the years of real love underneath the betrayal — must be rendered with the same commitment. The reader should feel both the comedy and the horror simultaneously, not in alternation.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is treated as a craft failure equivalent to a plot hole. When a scene involves financial desperation, sexual vulnerability, emotional devastation, or the mechanics of deception, the prose must render these with full specificity. 'She felt used' is never acceptable when you can show what being used feels and looks like in the body and in the specific details of the moment. The reader should not be protected from the characters' experiences.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices. If a line reads stiffly, the first fix is to contract.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters. After a high-intensity scene (confrontation, discovery, sexual encounter), the next chapter should breathe — slow down, change the sensory environment, let the character do something ordinary. Vary scene counts: some chapters are a single sustained scene, others are three or four. Vary dialogue density: some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are mostly interiority. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens in a location, the next should open in thought or in mid-conversation. The novel's middle section (chapters 8-13) should have more structural variety than the opening, which can afford to be more regular.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, educated, specific. No genre-thriller diction. No prestige-novel stiffness. The language should feel like how smart, funny, self-aware women in their mid-thirties actually think and speak. Cultural references are allowed when organic. Brand names when they carry social information. The prose should be able to be funny without stepping outside the character's perspective to wink at the reader.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person — very close, almost inhabiting the POV character's thought patterns and verbal rhythms. The prose should feel like the character's consciousness rendered in third person, not an external narrator describing the character. When in Nora's sections, the prose has her warmth and her specific noticing. When in Simone's sections, the prose has her edge and her accounting.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to mid-length sentences with natural cadence. Use compression for emotional peaks and psychological precision. Use longer, more digressive rhythms for scenes of intimacy, humor, and daily texture. Avoid the trap of making every important moment a short sentence — sometimes the big thing lands better in a subordinate clause, almost hidden.
